name: Hotjar
what_it_does: UX analytics platform with an API for accessing heatmap data, session recordings, survey responses, and user feedback to understand website visitor behavior and improve conversion.
use_cases: Exporting survey responses from Hotjar for automated sentiment analysis
Retrieving feedback widget responses to feed into customer intelligence pipelines
Accessing session and heatmap metadata for behavioral reporting
Triggering Hotjar surveys programmatically based on user actions
Correlating Hotjar feedback with other business data in agent-driven analysis
not_for: High-frequency event analytics (use Mixpanel or Amplitude)
Server-side behavioral tracking without browser session context
Enterprise-grade customer intelligence at scale (FullStory has deeper API)
best_when: You need an affordable heatmap and session recording tool with basic API access for exporting feedback and survey data into your own analysis workflows.
avoid_when: You need deep programmatic control over session recording data — Hotjar's API is limited compared to FullStory.

auth.notes: OAuth2 with API keys. API access requires Hotjar Business plan or higher. Keys generated in Hotjar Insights settings.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free Basic plan with 35 daily sessions, basic features. API access requires paid plan.
pricing.paid_tiers: Plus from $32/month; Business from $80/month (API access); Scale with custom pricing
pricing.notes: API access only on Business plan and above. Free tier useful for getting started but API integration requires paid subscription.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits not publicly documented; Business plan assumed moderate limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Hotjar's API documentation is less comprehensive than competitors. API surface is more limited — primarily designed for data export, not rich programmatic control.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read/export operations are idempotent. No write operations in the main API.
known_agent_gotchas: API is primarily for data export — cannot control recording behavior or trigger surveys programmatically via REST
Webhooks for feedback collection require Business plan
API documentation is sparse and some capabilities are only discoverable through support
Session recording URLs are not directly accessible via API — limited to metadata
Heatmap data export is aggregated, not per-session — cannot drill into individual user journeys via API
GDPR compliance means data may be anonymized in ways that limit analysis depth
